content: Borderline Personality Disorder (BPD) is characterized by pervasive instability in interpersonal relationships, self-image, affects, and marked impulsivity. Core features: frantic efforts to avoid abandonment, unstable/intense relationships (idealization/devaluation), identity disturbance, impulsivity in ≥2 self-damaging areas (spending, sex, substance use, reckless driving, binge eating), recurrent suicidal behavior/self-mutilation, affective instability, chronic emptiness, inappropriate intense anger, transient stress-related paranoia/dissociation. Prevalence ~1-3%, more commonly diagnosed in women. Pathophysiology involves emotional dysregulation, amygdala hyperreactivity, prefrontal cortex dysfunction, genetic factors (~40-60% heritability), and environmental factors (childhood trauma common).
